dsl_rule.translation_notes: Used SMA 20/50 crossover as primary trend-following indicator per signal specification of 20-period timeframe. ATR > 1.0 approximates abnormal price movement (>1 std dev). Regime shift detection implemented via moving average crossover. Exit conditions trigger on trend reversal signal (opposite crossover). Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to handle both long and short signals from same rule logic.

backtest_rule.reason: Used SMA 20/50 crossover as primary trend-following indicator per signal specification of 20-period timeframe. ATR > 1.0 approximates abnormal price movement (>1 std dev). Regime shift detection implemented via moving average crossover. Exit conditions trigger on trend reversal signal (opposite crossover). Direction set to CONTEXT_DEPENDENT to handle both long and short signals from same rule logic.

source_rule_text: A trader should use longer-term trend-following indicators to reduce transaction costs and capture abnormally large price moves that occur during major market shifts, rather than relying on shorter-term indicators that generate more frequent signals.
